VAL-SCENE-006.evidence: All existing SceneWriter methods (alloc_node, push_data, push_text_runs, push_shaped_glyphs, node_mut, commit, clear) and SceneReader methods (node, nodes, data, data_buf) are present in scene/lib.rs and exercised by existing tests in scene.rs. Tests include: writer_new_empty_state, writer_alloc_node_returns_sequential_ids, writer_alloc_node_initialized_to_empty, writer_node_mutation, writer_push_data_returns_valid_ref, writer_push_data_sequential_offsets, writer_set_root, writer_commit_increments_generation, writer_clear_resets_state, writer_text_node_round_trip, writer_text_runs_multiple_lines, push_text_runs_round_trips_struct_fields, shaped_glyph_single_run_round_trip, reader_reads_writer_output, reader_nodes_slice, reader_data_buf_slice, writer_build_typical_editor_scene, writer_replace_data_appends_new, writer_update_data_in_place, writer_update_data_wrong_length_fails, writer_reset_data_clears_usage, writer_node_overflow_returns_none, writer_data_overflow_truncates, writer_data_empty_push, reader_data_invalid_ref_returns_empty. All 1489 tests pass (0 failures) per parent validator.

VAL-SCENE-007.evidence: Test 'copy_then_mutate_preserves_other_nodes' in scene.rs: builds 8 well-known nodes (root, title_bar, title_text, clock_text, shadow, content, doc_text, cursor), swaps, snapshots front nodes, calls copy_front_to_back, mutates only cursor (node 7) x=100 y=48, swaps, then byte-compares all non-mutated nodes (0-6) against originals (asserts byte-identical). Also verifies cursor's first_child, next_sibling, width, and background are preserved despite x/y change. Change list verified to contain only node 7.

VAL-SCENE-008.evidence: Multiple tests cover change list overflow and sentinel behavior: (1) 'mark_changed_overflow_sets_full_repaint' — marks 25 nodes (> CHANGE_LIST_CAPACITY of 24), asserts change_count == FULL_REPAINT. (2) 'double_reader_full_repaint_on_overflow' — marks 25 nodes via DoubleWriter, asserts is_full_repaint() == true and change_list() returns None. (3) 'clear_sets_full_repaint' — verifies clear() (full rebuild) sets FULL_REPAINT sentinel. (4) 'mark_changed_after_overflow_is_noop' — further marks after overflow don't crash, sentinel preserved. (5) 'mark_changed_exact_capacity' — verifies 24 entries stored correctly, 25th triggers overflow. FULL_REPAINT constant defined as u16::MAX in lib.rs. DoubleReader::change_list() returns None on FULL_REPAINT, is_full_repaint() returns true.

VAL-SCENE-009.evidence: Four tests cover both exhaustion detection and in-place stability: (1) 'data_buffer_exhaustion_detectable' — fills data buffer above 75% threshold, copies front to back, asserts back inherits high data_used and threshold is detectable. (2) 'data_buffer_exhaustion_triggers_full_rebuild' — fills to >75%, demonstrates clear() + reset_data() fallback producing data_used=0 and FULL_REPAINT sentinel. (3) 'update_data_does_not_grow_data_used' — pushes data, runs 10 cycles of copy_front_to_back + update_data with same-length bytes, asserts data_used unchanged each cycle. (4) 'clock_update_data_used_unchanged' — full editor scene, incremental clock update via update_data on glyph data, asserts data_used unchanged. Source confirms update_data (lib.rs) overwrites in-place without modifying data_used, and replace_data/push_data grow data_used (bump allocator).

VAL-SCENE-010.evidence: Test 'copy_front_to_back_preserves_back_generation' in scene.rs: writes initial scene and swaps (front gen=1, back gen=0), calls copy_front_to_back, asserts front_generation() still 1, gets back buffer and asserts back_gen < front_gen (back gen remains 0, not overwritten with front's 1). Source code in lib.rs copy_front_to_back() explicitly saves back_gen before copy ('let back_gen = read_generation(self.buf, back_off)') and restores it after ('write_generation(self.buf, back_off, back_gen)'), preserving the double-buffer invariant.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. VAL-SCENE-006 confirmed by 25+ existing API tests all passing. VAL-SCENE-007 has dedicated copy-then-mutate test with byte-level comparison. VAL-SCENE-008 has 5 overflow/sentinel tests. VAL-SCENE-009 has 4 tests covering both threshold detection and in-place update stability. VAL-SCENE-010 has explicit test plus source code confirmation of generation preservation.
